t1.role: Bottom-line score. T1 must be stable.

t2.role: Main scoring zone. T2 modeling decides whether 200 is realistic.

t4.role: Strategy score. Take brute force and special properties without hurting T1/T2.

route: First protect T1 and T2. A 200-point route cannot tolerate unstable low-level mistakes.
Then train T3 partial-score decomposition. The goal is executable 30/50/70 routes, not immediate full marks.
Use T4 only as strategy training unless T1/T2 are already stable.
Every week must include one CSP-S-style set to verify transfer from single-problem practice to exam scoring.
